name: Remotion
why_it_matters: React-based video rendering is a natural fit for the existing Vite/React dashboard. It can turn our TV shots, subtitles, image clips and timing data into a real MP4 render pipeline.
risk: Large dependency and separate rendering mindset, but much cleaner than hand-writing complex FFmpeg timelines forever.
decision: Implement first as optional assembly renderer, not as replacement for the dashboard.

name: ComfyUI API Client candidates
why_it_matters: Our keyframe jobs already contain prompts, negative prompts, output paths and review targets. A ComfyUI client can turn those JSON jobs into real image batches.
risk: ComfyUI ecosystem changes often and clients vary in quality. Keep adapter thin and replaceable.
decision: Build our own tiny adapter around one client after local test.

name: StoryDiffusion / ComfyUI StoryDiffusion
why_it_matters: Character consistency across comic panels is one of our biggest problems. StoryDiffusion-style tools are relevant for same-character multi-shot generation.
risk: Research tooling may be heavy, unstable or too realistic for our cartoon style.
decision: Do not integrate into the dashboard yet. Keep as renderer experiment.

name: StoryMaker
why_it_matters: Potentially useful for keeping recurring characters stable, especially before or instead of LoRA training.
risk: May not match our exaggerated cartoon target and may require heavy setup.
decision: Research only until our core pipeline works.

name: Open TTS server candidates
why_it_matters: Our voice package already creates per-character lines and WAV targets. A local or API TTS server could render Falk, Rico, Kralle and Sami voices automatically.
risk: German voice quality and character acting may be weak. Human/ElevenLabs-style voices may still be better for final pilot.
decision: Add adapter later; first keep voice package renderer-neutral.

name: Subtitle generator candidates
why_it_matters: We already generate SRT/VTT from scripted lines. External subtitle tools are more useful after real voice audio exists.
risk: May be unnecessary because our script already knows the dialogue and rough timing.
decision: Do not integrate now. Our own SRT/VTT script is enough.

name: Generic video automation repos
why_it_matters: Useful for seeing how other projects glue FFmpeg, scripts and assets together, but most are not built for our character-consistent TV cartoon workflow.
risk: Could pollute our clean architecture with unrelated YouTube automation logic.
decision: Read for ideas only. Do not copy architecture.

name: Generic AI comic generators
why_it_matters: They prove the general market exists, but our system is more production-oriented: scene builder, TV shots, review, voice, sound and assembly.
risk: Most generic comic generators focus on one-off pages, not recurring TV-style episodes.
decision: Use as reference only. Keep our own pipeline.
